name: Liqueur De Framboise
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the berries and discard any that are overripe or moldy.
2. Place the berries in a large bowl and crush them slightly with the back of a wooden spoon.
3. In a medium saucepan, warm 2 cups of water and stir in the sugar until it is well dissolved and the liquid is just warm.
4. Pour the hot sugar syrup over the crushed berries and stir.
5. Cover the mixture with plastic wrap and refrigerate for one week, stirring occasionally.
6. After aging in the refrigerator, strain the mixture through a fine wire mesh strainer into a large bowl or aging container.
7. Add the alcohol mixture (2 cups pure grain alcohol and 2 cups water) to the strained berry liquid and stir.
8. Cap the container and let it age for one month.
9. Strain the mixture again through a cloth until it is clear.
10. Re-bottle as desired. The liqueur will be ready for use in cooking after this process, but it can continue to age for 2 months before drinking.
Variation: Instead of using pure grain alcohol and an additional cup of water, you may substitute 4 cups of 80 proof vodka. The resulting liqueur will have a slightly lower proof.
